cards.back: Bubble sort is a simple sorting algorithm that repeatedly steps through the list, compares adjacent elements, and swaps them if they are in the wrong order.
Selection sort is an in-place comparison-based algorithm in which the list is divided into two parts, the sorted part at the left end and the unsorted part at the right end.
Insertion sort is a simple sorting algorithm that builds the final sorted array (or list) one item at a time.

cards.front: What is Time complexity of Bubble Sort?
cards.back: The time complexity of bubble sort is O(n^2), where n is the number of items being sorted.

cards.front: What is Space complexity of Insertion Sort?
cards.back: The space complexity of insertion sort is O(1), meaning it requires constant additional space.
